prompt_context:
Databricks Certified Machine Learning Associate. Topics by weight:
- Databricks Machine Learning (38%): AutoML, MLflow tracking and registry, Unity Catalog for ML, Databricks ML runtimes, MLOps best practices
- Model Development (31%): data exploration, feature engineering, model training, hyperparameter tuning, evaluation metrics, model selection
- ML Workflows (19%): end-to-end pipeline design, feature stores, reproducible experiments, data preprocessing
- Model Deployment (12%): batch and real-time inference, deployment strategies, model serving
Pass mark: 70%. Format: 48 multiple-choice questions, 90 minutes. Question style: practitioner-level, scenario-based, focused on Databricks tooling.

domains.name: Databricks Machine Learning
domains.description: AutoML, MLflow tracking and registry, Unity Catalog for ML, Databricks ML runtimes, MLOps best practices

domains.name: Model Development
domains.description: Data exploration, feature engineering, model training, hyperparameter tuning, evaluation metrics, model selection

domains.name: ML Workflows
domains.description: End-to-end pipeline design, feature stores, reproducible experiments, data preprocessing

domains.name: Model Deployment
domains.description: Batch and real-time inference, deployment strategies, model serving on Databricks

prompt_context:
Databricks Certified Machine Learning Professional. Topics by weight:
- Experimentation (30%): tracking and versioning experiments, data preprocessing, feature engineering, designing reproducible ML experiments with MLflow
- Model Lifecycle Management (30%): full model lifecycle including training, versioning, model registry, CI/CD pipelines, MLflow and Unity Catalog integration
- Model Deployment (25%): production deployment patterns, custom model serving, model rollout strategies, scalable inference
- Solution and Data Monitoring (15%): detecting data drift, monitoring model performance in production, maintaining solution quality
Pass mark: 70%. Format: 60 multiple-choice questions, 120 minutes. Question style: advanced practitioner, scenario-based, assumes Associate-level knowledge.

domains.name: Experimentation
domains.description: Tracking and versioning experiments, data preprocessing, feature engineering, reproducible ML experiments with MLflow
